questions.question: UserAggregator проектируется с ValueState<UserSummary> + MapState<Long, WindowCounter> + ValueState<Long> lastOrderTime. Почему такая структура, не один большой ValueState<UserState>?

options.text: Different access patterns + TTL: UserSummary долгоживущий (90 days TTL для GDPR), WindowCounter короткоживущий (1 day TTL — windows старше не нужны), lastOrderTime — separate для clean velocity tracking. Один big ValueState означал бы либо short TTL (losing summary history) либо long TTL (огромный state). Decomposition позволяет per-component lifecycle

questions.explanation: State decomposition by lifecycle critical. Single big ValueState combining all data has worst-of-all-worlds TTL — choose long TTL (large state) или short TTL (lose persistent info). Decomposition: (1) UserSummary 90d TTL — cumulative metrics что нужно years; (2) MapState<window_start, WindowCounter> 1d TTL — windows старше day useless для real-time; (3) lastOrderTime separate ValueState — clean atomic update без serializing full summary. Per-state TTL = right size для each purpose. Pattern recommends для production stateful operators.

questions.question: Watermark alignment между Postgres CDC source и Kafka external source — зачем и как configure?

options.text: Без alignment fast source может убежать по event time, watermark всего pipeline застрянет на slow source. Configure: withWatermarkAlignment('main-pipeline-group', Duration.ofMinutes(2), Duration.ofSeconds(5)) на обоих sources с тем же group name. Max drift 2 min — fast source paused если разница больше

questions.explanation: Watermark alignment (FLIP-217) critical для multi-source pipelines. Problem: Kafka source может быть behind by minutes (consumer lag) while Postgres CDC source up-to-date. Без alignment: watermark = min(source watermarks) — stuck на Kafka. Temporal joins с future events from Postgres но stale dims from Kafka — incorrect. With alignment: same group + max-drift = fast sources paused if разница больше — все sources move together. Capstone: пауза Postgres CDC если Kafka lag > 2 min.

questions.question: Side outputs для fraud alerts vs anomaly events vs late events — почему separate тagger?

options.text: Different consumers + retention: fraud alerts → on-call Kafka topic с PagerDuty integration (critical, low volume); anomalies → separate alert topic (warnings); late events → archive topic for investigation (high volume, long retention для analysis). Mixing — pollutes consumers, hard to set per-stream retention/throughput
Side outputs не support multiple types

questions.explanation: Separation of concerns. Fraud alerts: high-priority, low-volume, immediate response required. Anomalies: medium-priority warnings. Late events archive: high-volume diagnostic data для investigation. Different Kafka topics:различные retention (alerts 30d vs archive 7d), throughput (10/sec vs 1000/sec), consumers (alerting service vs analyst tool), security (alerts restricted, archive analyst-accessible). Без separation everything goes to same place — noisy, hard to scale specific stream, security overlap. Pattern: один OutputTag = одна downstream concern.

questions.question: Fraud detection logic с complex ML inference в primary processElement — это правильный design для production fraud detection pipeline.
questions.explanation: Anti-pattern. Complex/slow fraud logic (ML inference, external API calls) в primary processElement blocks main pipeline throughput. Backpressure cascades, latency spikes для все events не just fraud-triggering. Production pattern: keep main path simple (basic rules: amount thresholds, velocity, fraud_score = quick computation), emit raw events на separate fraud pipeline (другой Flink job с ML_PREDICT inline). Fraud decisions returned through Kafka topic. Main pipeline остаётся fast, fraud pipeline can be scaled/upgraded independently. Это separation of concerns в architecture.
